title: Standard Wheels vs Tubeless Wheels Xiaomi m365 Scooter
body:
Many people have asked themselves this question. What is best for my scooter? We need to take several factors into account such as vibrations, energy savings, maintenance comfort and one of the most important, the possibility of punctures.

We have been able to test 3 types of wheels on the Xiaomi Mijia M365 scooter that you all know. Here you have a summary table of each of the factors that we have been able to perceive according to our experience.

[![Ruedas Tubeless Xiaomi m365 Scooter](https://patinetesya.com/wp-content/uploads/Captura-2.jpg)](https://patinetesya.com/wp-content/uploads/Captura-2.jpg)
> Ruedas Tubeless Xiaomi m365 Scooter

Let's analyze each of the elements that need to be taken into account. We will comment one by one on why we have given those scores to the factors.

## ENERGY SAVINGS

Perhaps this is the one we give the least importance to. Unless you are very tight on battery to your work or your usual place, one or two kilometers of autonomy doesn't matter that much. It is a factor that can be seen more in the long term.

For energy saving, it would be ideal if the wheels were inflated at the correct pressure, just as with a car. This problem does not exist in solid wheels. On the other hand, this wheel weighs considerably more than the others and that decreases the savings. Both the air chamber and tubeless need, according to our recommendations, to check their pressure at least once a week. If we always keep them at ideal pressure, we should get even more savings than with the solid one. But not everyone does this work, that's why they don't have the highest score.

## POSSIBILITY OF PUNCTURE

This one I would say is the most important. Followed by the vibrations you feel on your trips which we will discuss next. And here we need to stop more carefully. The chambers that for me would be perfect are the ones that come as standard, the ones with air chambers. The problem is that they puncture too much. In the end you're always thinking about whether that stone you just stepped on will puncture you. A puncture is not very expensive to fix, but it's a matter of not leaving us stranded. We need to have the assurance that we can go 20 kilometers and it won't leave us stranded halfway, something that has happened to me with air chambers.

This is where the tubeless wheel comes into play.

This type of wheel takes a long time to puncture. In fact, I haven't had one puncture yet even going through areas where with the air chamber I'm sure it would have left me stranded. Many will wonder why one punctures and the other doesn't if both have air inside. The technical differences will be explained below if anyone is curious.

Then we have the solid ones which obviously never puncture. It is the benefit that this type of wheels has, but you will see in the next point how it is not the only thing that matters.

## VIBRATIONS

### Comfort of vibrations

Let's move to the second most important point to consider about wheels, vibrations. Here we have two things to mention. First, those annoying vibrations when you go through slightly poorly paved streets or the type of cobblestone cause you to shake. It's so important to feel comfortable on long trips that I think a scooter with vibrations would eventually stop using it. After trying solid wheels, I think I've lost some bones along the way.

Wheels with air chambers, however, barely notice the imperfections. They have incredibly good absorption that makes it not matter the lack of a suspension system in this model. That's why I like these wheels so much, but we're back to before, if they leave me stranded then they're useless.

That leaves the tubeless wheels, which have absorption practically identical to wheels with chambers. Some people say they even feel the bumps less. In my personal case, I notice them a bit more, but nothing to worry about. You go very comfortably and you don't pay attention to these vibrations at any time.

### Scooter vibrations

The second thing that needs to be mentioned is the following. Perhaps someone might say it doesn't matter to them to notice these small bounces or that they accept them directly. Be careful here because not only your body vibrates. Logically the scooter absorbs all these micro vibrations and there have been many cases of loose cables, lights that don't work, chips that have been displaced or the battery has broken.

If you want to put solid ones, go first to the trusted repair shop and have them put silicone and some other things in strategic places to absorb the shocks. Then repairs end up being more expensive and it's something many people haven't taken into account and they tell us themselves.

###   
  
  
Miquel Badia  
  
  
  

## MAINTENANCE

Lastly, we have wheel maintenance. In any case, it is practically non-existent so it is not a decisive factor. Simply to have all elements in mind, the only thing worth noting is that on wheels with air chambers and tubeless we should check the pressure once a week. On solid wheels there is no maintenance other than checking if there is any breakage at any time.

## OTHERS

There are some other types of wheels that we are not talking about because we haven't tested them personally. We won't recommend or stop recommending anything we haven't had for a long period. An example of other alternatives to these three types of wheels would be those that are solid but with holes inside. As you can see in the image, this way, the wheel weighs somewhat less and absorbs shocks.

But without testing we won't recommend any of this. New types of wheels will come out and we will test them. We will update this page as the world of electric scooter tires advances.

## AIR CHAMBER VS Tubeless Wheels Xiaomi m365 Scooter

As I said before, many people don't understand why some puncture and others don't if both have air. For the curious, we will show how these wheels really work.

[![Ruedas Tubeless Xiaomi m365 Scooter](https://patinetesya.com/wp-content/uploads/tubeless.jpg)](https://patinetesya.com/wp-content/uploads/tubeless.jpg)
> Ruedas Tubeless Xiaomi m365 Scooter

As you can see in the image, we see the wheels simulating a cut in the middle of the tire and looking inside. On the left we can see how the air chamber fits onto the tire. On the left there is air, a bit more actually, but without a chamber. How can this be?

Tubeless tires can hold air inside without the help of a chamber because they are vacuum sealed. Also, with a special liquid a film is created that would simulate this chamber.

That's why tubeless tires need a tire, but not an air chamber. So the best thing is to reuse the ones that come as standard.

It's quite easy to understand, but very difficult to do. You can buy an air chamber, but you can't seal a wheel. We are trying to compile shops in all regions that do make tubeless wheels with experience.

CONCLUSION

If you have read all of the above you will have noticed for yourself that solid wheels are not the best option. Not comfortable, very heavy and in the end they damage the scooter. If we rule out this type of wheel, we are left with tubeless and air chamber wheels.

In summary, Xiaomi m365 Scooter Tubeless Wheels have all the benefits of air chamber wheels, but puncture much less. So yes, here there is no doubt, these are the wheels that I personally would recommend. I have used all types and today I go with these and very happy.
